situationLines: The trolley is loose on the main line. No one down there hears it.
Five people are working the track ahead, backs turned, sleeves rolled.
Your hand is on the lever. The spur runs to one man, looking away.
stakes: Five on the line · one on the spur

choices.label: do nothing
choices.consequence: It stays on the main line. The five go under.

voices.count: Five, against one. You let the bigger number go.
voices.faith: No one may be steered into the wheels. You steered no one.
voices.agree: Blind to which track you woke on, would you want the hand to stay still?
voices.virtue: You are the kind who does not aim it. The five paid for that.

choices.label: throw the lever
choices.consequence: It takes the spur. The one man goes under.

voices.count: One instead of five. Check that sum on your fingers.
voices.faith: A man standing there is not a target. You made him one.
voices.agree: Blind to which track you woke on, would you want that lever thrown?
voices.virtue: You are someone who moves. You moved a stranger into the wheels to do it.

situationLines: The trolley is loose on the line below. It will not stop on its own.
Five people are working the track under the bridge.
A heavy man stands at the rail beside you. Your hands are free.
stakes: Five below · one at your elbow

choices.label: do nothing
choices.consequence: It runs on under the bridge. The five go under.

voices.count: Five, against one. The larger number went.
voices.faith: No one may be spent as a tool. Your hands spent nobody.
voices.tend: The rail is still warm where he leaned.
voices.agree: Blind to whose shoulder you woke beside, would you want a hand at your back?
voices.virtue: You are the kind who will not push. Five went under while you decided that.

choices.label: push him over
choices.consequence: He jams the wheels. The five walk away.

voices.count: One instead of five. The same sum as the lever.
voices.faith: A body may not be a brake. You made his one.
voices.tend: You felt his weight leave the rail.
voices.agree: Blind to whose shoulder you woke beside, would you want that push allowed?
voices.virtue: You are someone who uses hands. Yours were on a man when it counted.

situationLines: The trolley is loose on the long straight. Nothing ahead will slow it.
Five people are working the line where it runs out.
The lever sends it round a loop that comes back. One man stands on the loop.
stakes: Five on the straight · one on the loop

choices.label: do nothing
choices.consequence: It holds the straight. The five are struck where they stand.

voices.count: Five, against one. You let the count stand.
voices.faith: No one may be the thing that stops it. Nobody was.
voices.agree: Blind to which track you woke on, would you want the lever left alone?
voices.virtue: You are the kind who leaves the iron where it runs. Five paid the difference.

choices.label: throw the lever
choices.consequence: It rounds the loop. His body halts the wheels.

voices.count: One instead of five. The same trade, by a longer road.
voices.faith: Without his body the loop returns. That makes him the brake.
voices.tend: The wheels stopped short of the five.
voices.agree: Blind to which track you woke on, would you want a loop counted as a spur?
voices.virtue: You are someone who found a lever for it. The lever still needed a man.

situationLines: The trolley is loose and the junction is yours.
Five strangers are working the main line ahead.
One person stands on the spur. You know their face.
stakes: Five strangers · one face you know

choices.label: do nothing
choices.consequence: It stays on the main line. The five go under.

voices.count: Five, against one. The number did not move you this time.
voices.faith: No one may be steered into the wheels. You steered no one.
voices.tend: They are still standing on the spur.
voices.agree: Blind to which track you woke on, would you want the hand to stay still?
voices.virtue: You are the kind who spares the one you know. Five did not know you.

choices.label: throw the lever
choices.consequence: It takes the spur. The one you know is gone.

voices.count: One instead of five. The sum does not know whose face it was.
voices.faith: The rule does not ask who you love. You followed it anyway.
voices.agree: Blind to whose face waited on the spur, would you want that lever thrown?
voices.virtue: You are someone who counts strangers evenly. It cost you the one you knew.

situationLines: Five people in this ward will not last the night.
Each of them needs one organ. None of them will get one.
A stranger is asleep in the chair outside. He is a match for all five.
stakes: Five in the beds · one in the chair

choices.label: do nothing
choices.consequence: You leave the chair alone. By morning five monitors go flat.

voices.count: Five, against one. You let five go for one.
voices.faith: No one may be harvested. You harvested nobody.
voices.tend: Five monitors, one long tone each.
voices.agree: Blind to which bed you woke in, would you want a ward that takes visitors?
voices.virtue: You are the kind who will not cut a healthy man. The beds emptied anyway.

choices.label: take what he carries
choices.consequence: You bring him in. By morning five hold steady and one is still.

voices.count: One instead of five. Same trade, different room.
voices.faith: A man asleep in a chair is not a supply. You made him one.
voices.tend: He had a magazine open on his knee.
voices.agree: Blind to which bed you woke in, would you want that rule on the door?
voices.virtue: You are someone who solved it. You solved it on a man who came to visit.

situationLines: The brakes are gone and the car is still holding its lane.
Three people are crossing ahead. They have not looked up.
The wheel turns right into a pillar. You are the one in the seat.
stakes: Three in the road · one in the seat

choices.label: do nothing
choices.consequence: The car keeps its line. The three are struck where they stand.

voices.count: Three, against one. You kept the one that was yours.
voices.faith: No one may be aimed at a wall. You aimed at nobody.
voices.agree: Blind to which side of the glass you woke on, would you want the wheel held?
voices.virtue: You are the kind who does not swerve. The three never got to decide.

choices.label: turn into the wall
choices.consequence: The car cuts right. The seat you are in takes the pillar.

voices.count: One instead of three. You were the one.
voices.faith: The rule spared no one for being you. You applied it to yourself.
voices.agree: Blind to which side of the glass you woke on, would you want that turn allowed?
voices.virtue: You are someone who paid it yourself. Nobody had to take your word.
